mnemonics: C.H.A.D. - Columnar storage is for Analytics (Heavy read, Aggregation, Dimensional queries), H.A.D.O.P. - Hadoop is for Archiving, Data Lakes, Offloading, Processing (unstructured data)
keyPoints: Data is stored in either Row-Based (OLTP) or Column-Based (OLAP) formats, each optimized for different operations.
Distributed File Systems (like HDFS) form the backbone of large-scale data storage in data lakes and big data platforms.
Data partitioning (e.g., by date) and clustering are critical techniques for improving query performance and manageability in a warehouse.
commonMistakes: Treating a data lake like a data warehouse by applying strict schema-on-write, which eliminates its flexibility for raw data storage.
Choosing the wrong storage format (e.g., using row-based for heavy analytical queries) leading to poor performance.
Under-partitioning data, resulting in full table scans for queries that could be limited to a small data subset.
